title: 10 best herbal creams for insect bites

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, work together to reduce redness and swelling caused by insect bites. Additionally, the cream's astringent qualities help to dry out the bite area, reducing itching and discomfort. As a result, pot marigold creams provide quick relief from the discomfort and irritation associated with insect bites, allowing individuals to resume their daily activities without distraction.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling, redness, and itching caused by insect bites, providing quick relief to affected areas.

Antiseptic: The antiseptic properties of pot marigold cream prevent infection and promote healing by killing bacteria and other microorganisms that may enter the bite wound, reducing the risk of secondary infections.
Antihistamine: Pot marigold's antihistamine property helps alleviate itching, irritation, and allergic reactions caused by insect bites, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling caused by insect bites.
Seeds: The seeds of the pot marigold plant are rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, which provide soothing relief from itching and inflammation associated with insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula flowers and dry them properly using a food dehydrator or air circulation method for 12 hours.
Grind 200g of dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground calendula powder with 300ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar, allowing it to infuse for 24 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and transferring the infused oil to a double boiler.
Add 10g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter to the double boiler, stirring until melted and well combined before pouring into a clean glass jar for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The soothing gel from the aloe vera plant calms redness, swelling, and itching caused by bug bites, providing instant relief. Additionally, aloe's antimicrobial agents help prevent infection by killing bacteria that can enter the wound through the bite. As a result, aloe creams effectively reduce pain, discomfort, and scarring associated with insect bites, making them an effective natural remedy for soothing itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, reduce swelling and redness caused by insect bites, while its antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate the skin response.
Soothing and calming effects: The gel of the aloe plant contains polysaccharides and glycoproteins that calm the skin, reducing itching, pruritus, and discomfort associated with insect bites.

Antimicrobial properties: Aloe vera's antimicrobial compounds, such as salicylic acid and cinnamic acid, help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can occur due to insect bites, preventing secondary infections and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds, leaves are used to calm redness, swelling, and itching caused by insect bites.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like aloenin and aloe-emodin, rhyzomes help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh aloe vera gel from the inner part of the leaf and wash it gently with cold water.
Mix 20ml of coconut oil with 10g of dried calendula flowers in a small saucepan over low heat for 30 minutes.
Combine the cooled aloe vera gel with the infused coconut oil mixture and add 5 drops of lavender essential oil.
Stir well to combine all ingredients and pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars with tight-fitting lids within an hour.
Allow the creams to mature at room temperature for 24 hours before using or storing them in a cool dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo creams helps with insect bites because of its potent antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as berberine and indole-3-carboxaldehyde, work together to reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by bug bites. These compounds also possess antimicrobial properties, which help prevent infection and promote the healing process. By applying wild indigo cream directly to the affected area, individuals can find relief from the discomfort and irritation associated with insect bites, allowing for a faster recovery and reduced risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild indigo contains compounds that reduce inflammation, swelling, and redness associated with insect bites, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antiseptic and antimicrobial properties: The cream's antiseptic and antimicrobial properties help to prevent infection by killing bacteria and other microorganisms that may enter the bite wound, reducing the risk of complications like abscesses or cellulitis.
Soothing and calming effects: Wild indigo has been traditionally used for its ability to calm irritated skin and reduce itching associated with insect bites, providing a sense of comfort and relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wild indigo plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites, making them an effective ingredient in topical creams.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes have antiseptic properties that prevent infection and promote healing when applied topically to bug bites, reducing the risk of secondary infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality baptisia tinctoria flowers and leaves in the late summer or early fall.
Dry the harvested material using a dehydrator on low heat for at least 24 hours.
Combine one part dried baptisia tinctoria with three parts coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining and discarding the solids.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil to create a smooth and creamy texture, allowing it to cool and solidify.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When applied topically, chamomile cream can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with insect bites. The natural antihistamine present in chamomile also helps to alleviate allergic reactions and prevent further inflammation. As a result, chamomile creams provide relief from the discomfort and pain caused by insect bites, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the urge to scratch or rub the affected area.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by insect bites, providing relief to affected skin.
Antiseptic: Chamomile's antiseptic properties help prevent infection and promote healing of bug bites, reducing the risk of bacterial or fungal infections.
Soothing agent: Chamomile's soothing properties calm irritated skin and reduce discomfort associated with bug bites, promoting a sense of relaxation and relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are a popular choice for making creams due to their high concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that helps soothe itchy skin caused by insect bites.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are often used in creams because they contain azulene, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that reduces swelling and redness associated with insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered chamomile with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass container.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to infuse the oil with the chamomile's active compounds.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 20% beeswax and 10% vitamin e oil, then melt at 60°c for 30 minutes to create a smooth and stable cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The cream contains compounds that help reduce swelling, redness, and itching caused by insect bites, providing quick relief from discomfort. Additionally, the cream's soothing effects calm irritated skin, promoting healing and reducing the risk of infection. As a result, stinging nettle creams offer a natural and effective solution for alleviating the discomfort and pain associated with insect bites.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites.

Antihistamine properties: Nettle creams have natural antihistamine properties, which help neutralize histamines released during an allergic response to bug bites, reducing redness, itching, and hives.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in nettle cream help dry up fluid-filled bug bites, such as welts and blisters, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Storing water and nutrients, nettle roots help soothe itchiness and reduce inflammation caused by insect bites.
Buds: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, nettle buds provide relief from itching, redness, and swelling associated with insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried nettle leaves and other desired herbs in suitable quantities for the recipe.
Combine 20g of dried nettle leaves with 10g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax has melted and the ingredients are well combined.
Remove from heat and add 2 tablespoons of olive oil and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil, stirring until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and set at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with insect bites because they contain anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's natural ingredients, such as allantoin and glycoside, work together to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with bug bites. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate and heal the affected area, promoting faster recovery and reducing the risk of infection. This makes plantain creams an effective and natural remedy for alleviating insect bite discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce swelling and redness caused by insect bites, providing quick relief from discomfort.
Antioxidant: Plantain's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals generated by insect venom, which can prevent further irritation and damage to the skin.

Soothing and moisturizing: The emollient properties of plantain cream soothe and calm irritated skin, while also providing long-lasting hydration and protection from environmental stressors that may exacerbate bug bite reactions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and itching from insect bites.
Barks: Containing astringent properties, they help dry out and reduce inflammation caused by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves and chop them finely into small pieces using scissors.
Combine the chopped leaves with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a double boiler setup on medium heat.
Heat the mixture for 20 minutes or until the plant material is fully infused and the oil turns greenish yellow.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bowl discarding the solids.
Mix 10 drops of tea tree essential oil with the infused oil and let it cool to room temperature before filling into jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The allantoin in comfrey soothes itching and reduces swelling, while its antibacterial compounds help prevent infection. Additionally, comfrey's antioxidant properties can calm redness and irritation caused by bug bites. By applying comfrey cream to affected areas, you can alleviate discomfort and promote healing, allowing you to recover quickly from those pesky insect bites.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Comfrey's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, help reduce swelling and redness caused by insect bites, making them an effective ingredient in soothing bug bite relief.

Antimicrobial properties: Comfrey's antimicrobial agents, including triterpenoid saponins and phenolic acids, work to combat bacterial infections that can occur due to insect bites, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.

Wound-healing properties: Allantoin, a key compound in comfrey, promotes collagen synthesis and tissue repair, helping to accelerate the healing process and reduce scarring associated with insect bites.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and redness caused by insect bites.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the comfrey plant contain allantoin, a compound that helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from itching and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried symphytum officinale leaves, 2 ounces of which will be needed for this recipe.
Combine the gathered herbs with 4 tablespoons of coconut oil in a clean glass jar and let it sit at room temperature for 4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth to separate the liquid from the solids, retaining 6 tablespoons of the liquid extract.
Melt 1/2 cup of beeswax pellets in a double boiler or in a heatproof bowl set over a pot of simmering water and let it cool slightly.
Combine the cooled beeswax mixture with the symphytum officinale extract, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 drops of tea tree essential oil, whipping until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with insect bites because of its natural anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as tannins and phenolic acids, help to reduce swelling, itching, and redness caused by insect bites. Witch hazel also has antihistamine-like effects, which can alleviate the intense itching associated with bug bites, providing quick relief from discomfort and promoting a faster healing process.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Witch Hazel's astringent properties help to reduce inflammation, itching, and redness caused by insect bites, by constricting blood vessels and drying up the affected area.

Antiseptic properties: The antiseptic properties of Witch Hazel cream help to prevent infection and promote wound healing by killing bacteria and other microorganisms that may be present on the skin after an insect bite.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Witch Hazel's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling, redness, and pain caused by insect bites, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing itchy bumps and welts.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of tannins, which help to reduce inflammation and itching caused by insect bites.
Barks: The barks have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to prevent infection and soothe the skin after an insect bite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh witch hazel leaves from the hamamelis virginiana plant, weighing about 50 grams.
Dry the witch hazel leaves in a warm and dark place for at least 7 days or until crumbly.
Grind the dried witch hazel leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, yielding approximately 20 grams.
Mix the powdered witch hazel with 100 milliliters of cooled boiled water to create a decoction, allowing it to steep for 10 minutes.
Combine the decoction with 200 grams of petroleum jelly and 50 grams of beeswax, melting the mixture in a double boiler while stirring until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that reduces swelling and itching caused by insect bites. Additionally, licorice root has natural antiseptic and antimicrobial agents that help prevent infection and promote healing. By applying herbal licorice cream to the affected area, it provides instant relief from discomfort, pain, and redness associated with insect bites, allowing for a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can reduce swelling and redness caused by insect bites and stings.
Antibacterial property: Herbal licorice creams often contain antibacterial agents like tea tree oil or aloe vera, which can prevent infection and promote healing of the affected area.
Sothing and calming property: Licorice root has anti-irritant properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin caused by insect bites, reducing itching and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing and calming skin irritations caused by insect bites.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant are used to create salves and creams that help to reduce itching, redness, and swelling associated with insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried licorice root extract glycyrrhiza glabra and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground licorice root powder with 50ml of cold-pressed coconut oil and 20ml of beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler and heat it gently over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax melts completely.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature, allowing the coconut oil to solidify into a creamy texture.
Transfer the cooled mixture to an air-tight container and store it in the refrigerator at 4°c for up to 6 months, or use immediately on affected areas as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with insect bites because they contain anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's natural ingredients, such as dandelion root and plantain, work together to reduce redness and swelling, making it an effective remedy for relieving itching and discomfort caused by mosquito bites, bee stings, and other insect bites.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by insect bites, promoting faster healing.
Saponins: The saponins present in dandelion creams have antimicrobial properties that help to combat bacterial infections often associated with insect bites, such as impetigo and cellulitis.
Triterpenoids: Dandelion triterpenoids, like taraxasterol, exhibit anti-pruritic (itch-relieving) effects, reducing the intense itching sensation caused by insect bites and promoting comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling caused by insect bites.
Barks: The barks of the dandelion plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to soothe itching and inflammation, providing relief from insect bite symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including dried taraxacum officinale roots 2 tablespoons, coconut oil 1 cup, beeswax 2 tablespoons and vitamin e oil 1 tablespoon.
Combine dried taraxacum officinale roots and coconut oil in a saucepan heating slowly over low heat for 4 hours stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding solids.
Mix beeswax with vitamin e oil in a separate small saucepan melting over low heat until smooth then slowly add to cooled taraxacum officinale infusion whisking constantly.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized glass jars allowing to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes before sealing tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herbal cream soothes the itching, redness, and swelling caused by insect bites, providing instant relief from discomfort. Its cooling effect also reduces heat and irritation, allowing the affected area to heal faster. Additionally, lemon verbena's antimicrobial properties prevent infection, making it an effective natural remedy for treating insect bites without harsh chemicals or artificial ingredients.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as citral and limonene, help reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites.
Antiseptic properties: The antimicrobial agents present in lemon verbena cream, including a-pinene and camphor, work to prevent infection and promote healing of bite wounds.
Astringent properties: Lemon verbena's astringent compounds, like limonoid, help reduce redness, itching, and inflammation by constricting blood vessels and drying out the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon verbena have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and preventing infection from insect bites.
Seeds: Lemon verbena seeds contain fatty acids and antioxidants that help soothe itchy skin and reduce inflammation caused by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried verbena officinalis leaves and 10 grams of coconut oil in a glass jar.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler for 30 minutes until it reaches 65°c, stirring occasionally.
Add the dried verbena officinalis leaves to the heated oil and let it steep for 60 minutes, shaking the jar every 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids, and store at room temperature for 24 hours.
Whip the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 2 grams of shea butter until it thickens and emulsifies, then package in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. When applied topically, it reduces redness, swelling, and itching caused by bug bites. The cream's soothing effects calm the skin, providing fast relief from discomfort and irritation. Additionally, St John's Wort's natural antimicrobial properties help prevent infection and promote healing. As a result, applying herbal St John's Wort creams can bring quick and gentle relief to itchy, bite-prone areas, making them an excellent remedy for summer bug bites.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic property: St John's Wort cream has antiseptic properties, which help to prevent infection and reduce the risk of secondary bacterial infections from insect bites.
Anti-inflammatory property: St John's Wort cream has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling, redness, and itching caused by insect bites.
Antipruritic property: St John's Wort cream has antipruritic properties, which help to soothe and relieve itching caused by insect bites, providing rapid relief from the discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and itching caused by insect bites.
Barks: The barks contain a high amount of hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in soothing insect bite symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried hypericum perforatum flowers at a dosage of 20 grams for every 100 milliliters of cream base.
Infuse the dried hypericum perforatum flowers in 100 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes and then strain.
Combine the infused hypericum perforatum liquid with 50 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of coconut oil at room temperature.
Stir the mixture until it reaches a consistency suitable for whipping into a creamy texture within 30 minutes.
Whip the mixture to incorporate air and achieve the desired cream consistency, taking approximately 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cornflower creams helps with insect bites because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The extracts from cornflowers contain flavonoids and saponins, which work together to reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by bites. The cream also has a calming effect on the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort. Additionally, cornflower's antiseptic properties help to prevent infection and promote healing, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating the discomfort associated with insect bites.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Cornflower cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with insect bites, providing relief from discomfort.

Antihistamine property: The antihistamine property in cornflower cream counteracts the allergic reaction caused by insect bites, reducing histamine release and alleviating symptoms such as itching, hives, and inflammation.
Antioxidant property: Cornflower cream's antioxidant property helps soothe and calm the skin, protecting it from further irritation and damage caused by insect bite-induced oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Cornflower roots have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing and calming itchy skin caused by insect bites.
Barks: The barks of the cornflower plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce redness and swelling associated with insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh centaurea cyanus flowers and leaves then dry them completely in shade or with low heat.
Combine 2 tablespoons dried flower and leaf mixture with 1 tablespoon coconut oil and 2 tablespoons beeswax in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low flame stirring constantly until the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches a smooth consistency.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool slightly then add 10 drops lavender essential oil and 5 drops tea tree essential oil.
Pour the cooled mixture into small glass jars filling them to the top and store them in a cool dry place for up to 2 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream's active compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, work to reduce redness and swelling caused by bug bites. Additionally, the antimicrobial effects help prevent infection and promote healing. This natural remedy provides a soothing and calming relief from itchy and painful insect bites, making it an effective alternative to harsh chemical-based creams or ointments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Joe Pye Weed contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which help reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites.

Antimicrobial activity: The cream's antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause infection and exacerbate insect bite reactions, promoting healing and preventing further irritation.
Soothing and calming effects: Joe Pye Weed's essential oils have a soothing and calming effect on the skin, which helps to reduce redness, itching, and discomfort associated with insect bites.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and preventing infection from insect bites.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and vitamins, they help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching caused by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eupatorium perfoliatum leaves and flowers in the early morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator or air circulation method for 24 hours.
Combine 10 grams of dried eupatorium perfoliatum with 20 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat until the beeswax melts, then allow it to cool and thicken for 30 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized jars and label accordingly, storing them at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with insect bites because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream soothes itchy skin, reducing redness and swelling caused by bug bites. Lemon balm's cooling effect also calms irritated areas, providing instant relief from discomfort. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help prevent infection and promote faster healing. As a result, applying lemon balm cream to insect bites can provide quick and effective relief from the discomfort and itchiness associated with these pesky bites.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Lemon balm cream's antihistamine properties help to reduce itching and inflammation caused by insect bites, allowing for faster healing and relief from discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm cream work to soothe and calm the skin, reducing swelling and redness associated with bug bites and stings.
Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm's antimicrobial properties help to prevent infection by killing bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can enter the wound through an insect bite or sting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, making them an effective ingredient in soothing and calming irritated skin caused by insect bites.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and redness associated with insect bites, allowing for faster healing and relief.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20g of dried melissa officinalis flowers and 100ml of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried herbs with 50ml of hot distilled water to create a decoction that steeps for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a separate container discarding solids and reserving liquid.
Mix the herbal liquid with 200ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler heating gently over low heat for 30-40 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool before transferring to glass jars filled to within an inch of the top.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marsh dandelion creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as taraxasterol and taraxin, work together to reduce swelling and itching caused by bug bites. Additionally, marsh dandelion's natural antioxidants help soothe the skin and promote healing. As a result, applying marsh dandelion cream to affected areas can provide rapid relief from discomfort and reduce the risk of infection. Its natural ingredients also make it an effective alternative to chemical-based creams and ointments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marsh dandelion creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which helps reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites.
Antihistamine properties: The cream's antihistamine compounds, such as sesquiterpenes, help alleviate allergic reactions to insect bites by blocking histamine release, reducing itching and redness.

Antimicrobial properties: The cream's antimicrobial compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help prevent infection by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause further complications from insect bites.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of taraxasterol, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in soothing and calming insect-bite reactions.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are employed for their ability to reduce itching, swelling, and redness associated with insect bites, thanks to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum palustre leaves measuring 50 grams in the early morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content to 5%.
Blend the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder and sift through a 100-mesh sieve to remove lumps.
Melt 200 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and mix with the powdered taraxacum palustre, adding 2% w/w of beeswax for thickening and stability.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and store in the refrigerator at 4°c to allow the ingredients to infuse for 14 days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream's active ingredient, angelica archangelica, has been shown to reduce swelling, itching, and redness associated with insect bites. It also contains soothing compounds that calm the skin, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing. Additionally, herbal angelica creams may help prevent infection by inhibiting bacterial growth, making them a natural and effective solution for relieving insect bite symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Angelica's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with insect bites, providing quick relief to affected areas.
Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of angelica cream can combat the bacteria introduced by an insect bite, preventing infection and promoting healing of the skin.
Analgesic: Angelica's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by insect bites, making it a natural remedy for soothing itchy and painful skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling caused by insect bites.
Barks: Containing bioactive compounds that help to reduce itching and inflammation, providing relief from itchy skin caused by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and dry them in shade for several days until they become brittle.
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using mortar and pestle or blender with 1 part by weight of angelica archangelica to 4 parts by weight of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Combine the ground angelica archangelica with beeswax in a double boiler and heat at low temperature for approximately 20 minutes until the mixture reaches a consistency of smooth cream.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth to remove any impurities and discard the solids retaining only the clear herbal infusion.
Fill sterilized glass jars with the prepared cream and store in refrigerator for up to one month or freeze for longer storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for insect bites are topical ointments made from natural herbs, essential oils, and plant extracts that provide relief from itching, redness, and inflammation caused by mosquito, bee, or other insect stings. These creams soothe and calm the skin, reducing discomfort and promoting faster healing. Examples of herbal creams that help with insect bites include aloe vera, tea tree oil, and calendula cream. By using these creams, people can alleviate the itchy annoyance of bug bites, allowing them to enjoy outdoor activities without distraction, and improving their overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat insect bites with herbal creams is to apply a soothing mixture of herbs such as calendula, chamomile, and plantain directly to the affected area. These natural remedies help reduce inflammation, ease itching, and promote healing. Aloe vera gel or witch hazel can also be added to calm irritated skin. Apply the cream topically 2-3 times a day for effective relief from insect bite symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to insect bites that are treated with herbal creams are skin irritations, rashes, and minor burns. Herbal creams can provide relief from symptoms such as itching, redness, and swelling. For example, creams containing aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin, while creams with capsaicin or menthol can reduce pain and itching associated with minor burns and sunburns.
